Simplify \(\dfrac{\sqrt{b}}{\sqrt{a}-\sqrt{b}}+\dfrac{\sqrt{a}}{\sqrt{a}+\sqrt{b}}\).
A. \(\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{a}-\sqrt{b}}\)
B. \(\dfrac{a+2\sqrt{ab}-b}{a-b}\)
C. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{b}+\sqrt{a}}{2\sqrt{a}}\)
D. \(\dfrac{b+2\sqrt{ab}-a}{a-b}\)
E. \(\dfrac{a+b}{a-b}\)

Find the greatest value of \(3x+2y\) if \((x,y)\) is a point lying in the region \(OABCD\) (including the boundary).

A. \(15\)
B. \(13\)
C. \(12\)
D. \(9\)
E. \(8\)

The diagram shows the graphs of \(y=ax^2+bx\) and \(y=cx+d\). The solutions of the equation \(ax^2+bx=cx+d\) are

A. \(-1, 1\).
B. \(-1, 2\).
C. \(0, 1\).
D. \(0, 3\).
E. \(1, 3\).

If \(\log(p+q)=\log p+\log q\), then
A. \(p=q=1\).
B. \(p=\dfrac{q}{q-1}\).
C. \(p=\dfrac{q}{q+1}\).
D. \(p=\dfrac{q+1}{q}\).
E. \(p=\dfrac{q-1}{q}\).

The expression \(x^2-2x+k\) is divisible by \((x+1)\). Find the remainder when it is divided by \((x+3)\).
A. \(1\)
B. \(4\)
C. \(12\)
D. \(16\)
E. \(18\)

Find the H.C.F. and L.C.M. of \(ab^2c\) and \(abc^3\).
A. H.C.F. \(a\), L.C.M. \(a^2b^3c^4\).
B. H.C.F. \(abc\), L.C.M. \(ab^2c^3\).
C. H.C.F. \(abc\), L.C.M. \(a^2b^3c^4\).
D. H.C.F. \(ab^2c^3\), L.C.M. \(abc\).
E. H.C.F. \(a^2b^3c^4\), L.C.M. \(abc\).

If \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are the roots of the quadratic equation \(x^2-3x-1=0\), find the value of \(\dfrac{1}{\alpha}+\dfrac{1}{\beta}\).
A. \(-3\)
B. \(-1\)
C. \(-\dfrac{1}{3}\)
D. \(\dfrac{2}{3}\)
E. \(3\)

If the simultaneous equations \(\begin{cases} y=x^2-k \\ y=x \end{cases}\) have only one solution, find \(k\).
A. \(-1\)
B. \(-\dfrac{1}{4}\)
C. \(-4\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{4}\)
E. \(1\)

The price of a cylindrical cake of radius \(r\) and height \(h\) varies directly as the volume. If \(r=5 \text{ cm}\) and \(h=4 \text{ cm}\), the price is \( \$ 30 \). Find the price when \(r=4 \text{ cm}\) and \(h=6 \text{ cm}\).

A. \( \$ 25 \)
B. \( \$ 28.80 \)
C. \( \$ 31.50 \)
D. \( \$ 36 \)
E. \( \$ 54 \)

A. \(2.25 \text{ cm}\)

D. \(4.5 \text{ cm}\)
E. \(6 \text{ cm}\)

A. \(24:\pi\)
B. \(3:1\)
C. \(6:\pi\)
D. \(3:\pi\)
E. \(8:3\pi\)

The figure shows a solid consisting of a cylinder of height \(h\) and a hemisphere of radius \(r\). The area of the curved surface of the cylinder is twice that of the hemisphere. Find the ratio volume of cylinder : volume of hemisphere.

A. \(1:3\)
B. \(2:3\)
C. \(3:4\)
D. \(3:2\)
E. \(3:1\)

A merchant marks his goods \(25\%\) above the cost. He allows \(10\%\) discount on the marked price for a cash sale. Find the percentage profit the merchant makes for a cash sale.
A. \(12.5\%\)
B. \(15\%\)
C. \(22.5\%\)
D. \(35\%\)
E. \(37.5\%\)

\(\dfrac{\cos\theta}{1-\sin^2\theta}\times\dfrac{1-\cos^2\theta}{\sin\theta}=\)
A. \(\sin\theta\)
B. \(\cos\theta\)
C. \(\tan\theta\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{\sin\theta}\)
E. \(\dfrac{1}{\cos\theta}\)

A. \(0\)
B. \(1\)
C. \((1-\sin^2\theta)^2\)
D. \((1-\cos^2\theta)^2\)
E. \((\cos^2\theta-\sin^2\theta)^2\)

A. \(1\).

D. \(3\).

If the points \((1,1), (3,2)\) and \((7,k)\) are on the same straight line, then \(k= \)
A. \(3\).
B. \(4\).
C. \(6\).
D. \(7\).
E. \(10\).

\(A(0,0), B(5,0)\) and \(C(2,6)\) are the vertices of a triangle. \(P(9,5), Q(6,6)\) and \(R(2,-9)\) are three points. Which of the following triangles has/have area(s) greater than the area of \(\Delta ABC\)?
I. \(\Delta ABP\)
II. \(\Delta ABQ\)
III. \(\Delta ABR\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

A circle of radius \( 1 \) touches both the positive \(x\)-axis and the positive \(y\)-axis. Which of the following is/are true?
I. Its centre is in the first quadrant.
II. Its centre lies on the line \(x-y=0\).
III. Its centre lies on the line \(x+y=1\).
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. I and III only

What is the area of the circle \(x^2+y^2-10x+6y-2=0\)?
A. \(32\pi\)
B. \(34\pi\)
C. \(36\pi\)
D. \(134\pi\)
E. \(138\pi\)

Two fair dice are thrown. What is the probability of getting a total of \( 5 \) or 10?
A. \(\dfrac{1}{9}\)
B. \(\dfrac{5}{36}\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{6}\)
D. \(\dfrac{7}{36}\)
E. \(\dfrac{2}{9}\)

A group of \(n\) numbers has mean \(m\). If the numbers \(1\), \(2\) and \( 6 \) are removed from the group, the mean of the remaining \(n-3\) numbers remains unchanged. Find \(m\).
A. \(1\)
B. \(2\)
C. \(3\)
D. \(6\)
E. \(n-3\)

The figure shows the frequency polygons of two symmetric distributions \(A\) and \(B\) with the same mean. Which of the following is/are true?
I. Interquartile range of \(A \lt \) Interquartile range of \(B\)
II. Standard deviation of \(A \gt \) Standard deviation of \(B\)
III. Mode of \(A \gt \) Mode of \(B\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

From the table, a root of the equation \(f(x)=0\) is
A. \(3.57\) (correct to \( 3 \) sig. fig.).
B. \(3.575\) (correct to \( 4 \) sig. fig.).
C. \(3.5775\) (correct to \( 5 \) sig. fig.).
D. \(3.5725\) (correct to \( 4 \) sig. fig.).
E. \(3.58\) (correct to \( 3 \) sig. fig.).

Given that the positive numbers \(p, q, r, s\) are in G.P., which of the following must be true?
I. \(kp, kq, kr, ks\) are in G.P., where \(k\) is a non-zero constant.
II. \(a^p, a^q, a^r, a^s\) are in G.P., where \(a\) is a positive constant.
III. \(\log p, \log q, \log r, \log s\) are in A.P.
A. I only
B. II only
C. I and II only
D. I and III only
E. I, II and III only

B. \(\sqrt{34} \text{ cm}\)
C. \(7\text{ cm} \)
D. \(\sqrt{226} \text{ cm}\)
E. \(\sqrt{241} \text{ cm}\)

In factorizing the expression \(a^4+a^2b^2+b^4\), we find that
A. \((a^2-b^2)\) is a factor.
B. \((a^2+b^2)\) is a factor.
C. \((a^2-ab-b^2)\) is a factor.
D. \((a^2-ab+b^2)\) is a factor.
E. it cannot be factorized.

If the solution of the inequality \(x^2-ax+6\le 0\) is \(c\le x\le 3\), then
A. \(a=5, c=2\).
B. \(a=-5, c=2\).
C. \(a=5, c=-2\).
D. \(a=1, c=-2\).
E. \(a=-1, c=2\).

B. \(\dfrac{1}{3}\).
C. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{8}\).
D. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\).
E. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\).

B. \(\dfrac{2}{5}\).
C. \(\dfrac{9}{25}\).
D. \(\dfrac{16}{25}\).
E. \(\dfrac{21}{25}\).

Which of the following gives the compound interest on \( \$ 10 \) 000 at \( 6 \% \) p.a. for one year, compounded monthly?
A. \(10\,000 \times \dfrac{0.06}{12} \times 12\)
B. \(10\,000(1.06^{12}-1)\)
C. \(10\,000\left(1+\dfrac{0.06}{12}\right)^{12}\)
D. \(10\,000\left[\left(1+\dfrac{0.06}{12}\right)^{12}-1\right]\)
E. \(10\,000\left[\left(1+\dfrac{0.6}{12}\right)^{12}-1\right]\)

Originally \(\dfrac{2}{3}\) of the students in a class failed in an examination. After taking a re-examination, \( 40 \% \) of the failed students passed. Find the total pass percentage of the class.
A. \(26\dfrac{2}{3}\%\)
B. \(33\dfrac{1}{3}\%\)
C. \( 40 \% \)
D. \( 60 \% \)
E. \(73\dfrac{1}{3}\%\)
